program_en: B.B.A. in Hospitality and Real Estate
program_chi: 酒店旅遊及房地產工商管理學士
learning_outcomes:
Hospitality and Real Estate 1. Major Programme: Business concepts and functions:
Graduates should have mastered
different business concepts and functions so that they can lead a hospitality
enterprise as an integrated business firm. They should have possessed
broad-based core knowledge of general management covering accounting,
finance, marketing, human resources, information technology, etc. At the same
time, graduates should have mastered a deep understanding of the context of
managing hospitality and tourism businesses. Analytical skills: Students should have mastered
analytical skills to identify a business problem, generate and compare
alternatives, and come up with a prudent solution. Students will have
developed managerial skills such as communication, problem solving, analytical,
critical thinking and decision making, etc throughout the curriculum. In this
regard, graduates should possess a core level of generic skills related to
management of hospitality businesses, including: Computer literacy/IT skills,
quantitative skills and research methods, and decision making skills. Professional Skills: Graduates should have a mastery
of professional skills pertaining to management of business in service
industry. They should be able to operate different hospitality and tourism
organizations. They should be able to identify current hospitality and
tourism management problems and propose appropriate solutions. Attitude/Values: Graduates should have a positive
attitude and passion toward the hospitality and tourism industry. They should
be nurtured servant-leadership. They should have possessed values for
excellence, leadership, creativity, service and ethics in professional and
personal life. They should have developed an attitude for adapting to the
changing social and environment and become an effective and responsible
citizen.
study_scheme:
Hospitality and Real Estate Hospitality and Real Estate Applicable to students admitted in 2024-25
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 73 units of
courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Faculty Package:  |  9
ACCT1111, DOTE1031, HTMG1010
2.  |  Required Courses:  |  37
DOTE1021, 2051#,
FINA2010#, HTMG1091, 1092, 2000, 2070, 2091, 2092, 2900, 3010, 3030, 3041,
3091, 3092, 3900, 4091, 4092, 4800, 4900 (capstone course), MKTG2010#
3.  |  Stream Courses:  |  15
Students are required to choose one
of the following streams:
(a)  |  Hospitality
HTMG3020, 3060, 3521, 3527, 4600 (with
a topic in Hospitality)
To further strengthen the stream
area, students are recommended to take MGNT 4050, 4060, 4140, 4540, MKTG3020, 3030, 4040, 4080 for free electives.
(b)  |  Real Estate
HTMG3502, 3523,
4100, 4190, 4600 (with a topic in Real Estate)
To further
strengthen the concentration area, students are recommended to take ECON3440, FINA3010, 3060, 3080, GRMD2105
for free electives.
4.  |  Elective Courses:  |  12
(a)  |  Students are required to complete 9
units from the three modules below:
(i)  |  Analytical Module:
HTMG3504, 3522, 3525, 3526, 4060,
4230, 4250, 4270
(ii)  |  Innovative Module:
DOTE3010#, HTMG3050, 3519, 3524,
4130, 4200, 4240, 4260, MGNT2611#
(iii)  |  Experiential Module:
HTMG3501, 3508, 3515, 3518, 3520,
4120, 4170, 4280, 4700
(b)  |  Students are required to complete at
least 3 units of courses from below:
HTMG3590, 4501,
4502, 4503, 4504, 4505, 4506, 4508, 4509, 4510, 4511, 4512, 4513, 4516,
4517, 4519, 4520, 4521, 4522, 4523, 4524, 4526, 4527, 4528, 4529, 4530,
4531, 4532, 4533, 4534, 4535, 4536, 4537, 4538, 4539
Total:  |  73

1.  |  HTMG courses
at 2000 and above level, excluding HTMG2000, as well as those labeled as
# will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
